subject: english
teaching_goal: Analyze common problems students face in listening comprehension; apply effective strategies to improve students' listening skills; compare the effects of different teaching methods on listening improvement.
transcript:
[0.0 -> 15.78] Good afternoon dear teachers and judges,
[15.78 -> 61.02] it's my great honor to present my impromptu speech here whose title is How to Improve Students' Listening Skills. Well when I was doing my internship in the middle school many students would have this kind of problem in listening they just cannot understand the meaning of what the speakers say and they might come across the problems of American accent and British accent well as to solve this problem I have applied many many many strategies and today I'd like to share with you guys and the first is before listening before listening we usually I just find two ways. One is the bottom-to-up ways. What is bottom-to-up?

visual_narrative: The introduction phase begins with the teacher standing confidently in front of a screen displaying the hook object: "The Virtual Chorus" with the subtitle "Sound of Physics." The teacher gestures towards the screen, drawing attention to the title, which serves as the initial visual stimulus. The classroom setting is organized, with students seated and focused on the presentation. The teacher uses expressive hand movements to engage the audience, occasionally pointing to the screen to emphasize the topic. The transition from the hook to the main topic is seamless, as the teacher maintains eye contact with the students while elaborating on the concept of sound in physics. The visual aid remains consistent, reinforcing the theme of the virtual chorus. Throughout the introduction, the teacher's gestures and positioning create a connection between the new knowledge and the students' existing understanding of sound, setting the stage for a deeper exploration of the physics involved. This structured approach ensures that the introduction not only captures interest but also provides a clear pathway for future learning.
